twitter_thread: 70% of AI projects are being axed due to costs. One company cut $1.2M in AI spending by switching to Hugging Face.
Rationing AI means ditching expensive cloud services for open-source tools like TensorFlow, saving up to 40% on infrastructure costs.
Cutting named entity recognition can save $50k/month, but may decrease data quality by 15% due to lost insights.
The average company uses 5 AI tools, but successful rationing requires cutting to 2-3 essential tools, like chatbots and predictive analytics.
Companies that measure ROI on AI projects see a 25% increase in efficiency. This requires a 10% boost in data analytics staff to track metrics.
Review AI projects every 3 months to ensure they're still necessary and cost-effective, or risk wasting $100k/month on redundant projects.
What's the most surprising AI feature you've had to cut due to cost, and how did you adapt? #airaion #techcosts
pack.linkedin: 70% of AI projects are being put on hold due to soaring costs. To adapt, companies like IBM are turning to open-source tools, saving up to 40% on infrastructure costs. However, this can lead to a 15% decrease in data quality if not done carefully. Measuring ROI on AI projects is key, with companies seeing a 25% increase in efficiency. What steps has your company taken to ration AI, and what were the results?
tiktok_hooks: One company cut $1.2M in AI spending by switching to open-source tools
70% of AI projects are being put on hold due to costs, what's your company doing?
AI costs are skyrocketing, with some companies wasting $100k/month on redundant projects
pack.reddit_headline: Is Rationing AI the Only Way to Survive the $1.2M Question: Soaring Machine Learning Costs?
